title: Malty Velvety Syrian Potato Bao
description: A sweet and savory pastry filled with a malty, velvety potato filling, perfect for a festive Syrian dinner.
instructions: Combine warm water, sugar, and yeast to activate yeast in a large mixing bowl.
Add flour, oil, and egg to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Divide dough into 6-8 equal pieces and roll into balls.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle and place in a greased pan.
Spread 2-3 tablespoons of potato filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press edges to seal.
Place buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush tops with egg wash and sprinkle with pumpkin pie spice.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown.
While buns are baking, prepare the potato filling by boiling diced potatoes until tender.
Mash boiled potatoes with butter, malt syrup, rose water, and salt.
Add pumpkin pie spice to taste and mix well.
Let buns cool for 5 minutes before serving.
